DRAUGEN.formation: Rogn Formation (Late Jurassic, main reservoir); Garn Formation (Middle Jurassic, western part)
DRAUGEN.api_gravity_blend_source: Equinor crude oil assay (updated 27.08.2024) - Draugen blend
DRAUGEN.sulfur_note: Equinor characterizes as 'Light, low sulphur crude oil' from Norway
DRAUGEN.ooip_note: STOOIP estimate for Garn Formation (does not include Rogn main reservoir)
DRAUGEN.recovery_mechanism: Natural aquifer pressure support + water injection from south
sources: https://archives.datapages.com/data/specpubs/fieldst2/data/a014/a014/0001/0350/0371.htm (AAPG Datapages: Draugen Oil Field, Haltenbanken Province)

BRAGE.location: Northern North Sea, ~120km NW of Bergen, 10km E of Oseberg
BRAGE.formation: Statfjord Group (Lower Jurassic), Brent Group (Middle Jurassic), Fensfjord Formation (Middle Jurassic), Sognefjord Formation (Upper Jurassic)
BRAGE.api_gravity_source: Field-level oil gravity reported in field profile
BRAGE.api_gravity_statfjord_note: Statfjord formation: highly undersaturated 36 API oil (SPE-50641-MS Brage 5-year lessons learned)
BRAGE.api_gravity_fensfjord_note: Fensfjord oil has similar properties as Statfjord
sources: https://onepetro.org/SPEEURO/proceedings-abstract/98EUROPEC/98EUROPEC/SPE-50641-MS/191099 (SPE-50641: Brage Field, Lessons Learned After 5 Years)

YME.operator_history: Repsol (current per most sources); OKEA holds equity
YME.operator_okea_note: Yme is sometimes listed in OKEA portfolio context but is Repsol-operated; verify license shares for OKEA equity
YME.location: Egersund Basin, southern Norwegian North Sea
YME.reservoir_structures: Gamma and Beta main structures, six deposits
YME.reservoir_pressure_note: Low reservoir pressure - requires artificial lift initially and after water breakthrough
YME.asphaltene_note: High asphaltene content in crude oil (challenge)

GJOA.operator_okea_note: OKEA holds equity (12% partner stake); not operator. Listed in OKEA portfolio.
GJOA.location: Northern North Sea, ~50km NE of Troll
GJOA.formation: Dunlin Group, Brent Group, Viking Group (Jurassic sandstone)
GJOA.reservoir_temperature_note: 129.4°C in DST1 zone (exploration well 2/4-5 area); may not represent Gjøa producing reservoir directly
GJOA.api_gravity_note: 37.3° API reported from exploration well DST; field is gas-cap above thin oil zone
GJOA.gor_note: 236 Sm3/Sm3 from exploration well DST; producing reservoir GOR may differ
GJOA.fluid_type: Gas above relatively thin oil zone
GJOA.reservoir_quality: Several tilted fault segments with partly uncertain communication, variable reservoir quality

okea_portfolio_note: OKEA operates Draugen and Brage. OKEA is partner (non-operator) in Yme (Repsol-operated) and Gjøa (Vår Energi-operated, formerly Neptune Energy). OKEA also has interests in Statfjord area, Nova, Yggdrasil area, Hasselmus (gas tieback to Draugen).

key_findings.lightest_oil: Draugen blend at 41.4 API (Equinor assay) - light, low sulfur
key_findings.intermediate: Yme at 38-39 API, 0.17% S - medium-light sweet
key_findings.gjoa_complex: Gas-condensate field with thin oil rim, 37.3 API from exploration
key_findings.heaviest: Brage at 33.7 API field-level (Statfjord segment 36 API), 0.37% S

sources_consulted: SPE-50641-MS (Brage Field, Lessons Learned After 5 Years of Production, EUROPEC 1998)
AAPG Datapages - Draugen Oil Field, Haltenbanken Province
Sodir factpages (well 2/4-5 exploration DST for Gjøa area)
